title: PhD
biography: Har Gobind Khorana was an Indian-American biochemist who shared the Nobel Prize in 1968 for interpreting the genetic code and its function in protein synthesis. He was the first to synthesize a functional gene and made foundational contributions to understanding how nucleotide sequences determine amino acid assembly.
aiSummary: Har Gobind Khorana was a molecular biology specialist at MIT who decoded the genetic code. Nobel Prize 1968 for interpreting the genetic code. Based in Cambridge, United States.

affiliations.role: Alfred P. Sloan Professor of Biology and Chemistry

citations.title: Studies on polynucleotides: the synthesis of the ribonucleoside-3',5'-cyclic phosphates

awards: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (1968)

timeline.title: Began genetic code experiments
timeline.description: Synthesized specific RNA sequences to determine which codons encode which amino acids

timeline.title: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine
timeline.description: Shared with Robert Holley and Marshall Nirenberg for interpreting the genetic code

timeline.title: First artificial gene synthesis
timeline.description: Synthesized the first functional artificial gene

timeline.title: Joined MIT
timeline.description: Appointed Alfred P. Sloan Professor of Biology and Chemistry at MIT
